name: Pralines and Variations
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine sugars, milk, vanilla extract, and butter in a saucepan.
2. Bring mixture to boil over high heat.
3. Add pecans. Continue boiling until the mixture reaches the soft-ball stage of 238 to 240°F, stirring constantly.
4. Test for the soft-ball stage by dropping a spoonful into a cup of cold water. If it clings together in a ball, it is ready.
5. Remove from heat and stir until the mixture thickens, becomes creamy and cloudy, and the pecans remain suspended.
6. Spoon out onto buttered wax paper, foil, or parchment paper. When using wax paper, ensure to buffer with newspaper underneath, as hot wax will transfer to whatever is beneath.
Variations:
- Chocolate Pralines: Just before the praline mixture reaches the soft-ball stage, remove from heat and add 1/2 cup semisweet chocolate chips. Stir until mixed well.
- Peanut Butter Pralines: Just before the praline mixture reaches the soft-ball stage, remove from heat and add 1/3 cup peanut butter. Stir until mixed well.
- Chocolate-Peanut Butter Pralines: Just before the praline mixture reaches the soft-ball stage, remove from heat and add 1/4 cup semisweet chocolate chips and 1/6 cup peanut butter. Stir until mixed well.
